# Employes blackbox analyse {#employes-blackbox-analyse}

**BVA and equivalence partitions for employee fields**

The minimum length for names is 1, for example the Danish name Ø.  
The minimum length for an e-mail is 5 because it needs to consist of a letter, followed by a @ and a domain. Minimum domain length is 3\. Example: a@a.dk

| Value | Partition type | Partitions | Test case values | Boundary values | Test case values |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Email length | Invalid | 0-4 | 3 | 0 4 | 0 1 3 4 5 |
|  | Valid | 5 \- 320 | 150 | 5 320 | 4 5 6 319 320 321 |
| Password length | Invalid | 0-7 | 4 | 0 7 | 0 1 6 7 8 |
|  | Valid | 8-255 | 125 | 8 255 | 7 8 9 254 255 256 |
|  | Invalid | 256-MAX INT | 300 | 256 | 255 256 257 |
| First name \+ last name length | Invalid | 0 | 0 | 0 | 0 1 |
|  | Valid | 1-100 | 50 | 0 100 | 0 1 2 99 100 101 |
|  | Invalid | 101-MAX INT | 150 | 101 | 100 101 102 |

## First Name & last name {#first-name-&-last-name}

Starts with capital letter, 1 word, no whitespace

### Decision Table {#decision-table}

Minimum length name is based on the Danish name Ø.

| Case | Empty? | Letters only? | Result | Test value |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | No | Yes | Valid | Jensen |
| 2 | Yes | \- | Invalid | ““ |
| 3 | No | No (numbers) | Invalid | Jensen1 |
| 4 | No | No (symbols) | Invalid | Jensen@ |
| 5 | No | No (space) | Valid | Jensen Jens |

### Password black box tests {#password-black-box-tests}

The password must be at least 8 characters and can at most be 64 characters long.  
Must contain at least one of each:  
Lowercase letter, an uppercase letter, a number

**Section 5.1**: Password length 8–64 characters, with at least one lowercase, one uppercase, one number.

**FR-EMP-10**: Passwords must be hashed before storage.

###  **Decision table** {#decision-table-1}

| Case | Length 8 | Uppercase | Lowercase | Number | Test | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | False | \- | \- | \- | pass | Error |
| 2 | \- | False | \- | \- | password | Error |
| 3 | \- | \- | False | \- | PASSWORD | Error |
| 4 | \- | \- | \- | False | Password | Error |
| 5 | True | True | True | True | Passw0rd | Accepted |

**Email decision table:**

E-mails follow the format of length 320 and has a valid local part and domain, one @. We don’t verify the domain exists or the TLD. Just that the TLD is at least 2 letters long and the domain is at least one letter long.

**FR-EMP-06**: Each employee must have a unique email address.Since uniqueness is a boolean state (exists / doesn't exist), a simple decision table is sufficient.

* R0: Length is above 4 and below 321  
* R1: Contains exactly one @  
* R2: Valid local part format (Part prior to @)  
* R3: Valid domain format (at least 1 letter \+ includes dot \+ 2 letters)  
* R4: email is unique

| Case | R0 | R1 | R2 | R3 | R4 | Test | Result |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 1 | T | T | T | T | T | a@a.dk | Valid |
| 2 | \- | F | \- | \- | \- | @@das | Invalid |
| 3 | F | \- | \- | \- | \- | a@a | Invalid |
| 3 | \- | \- | F | \- | \- | @aaa.dk | Invalid |
| 4 | \- | \- | \- | F | \- | aaa@a | Invalid |
| 5 | \- | \- | \- | \- | F | a@a.dk | Invalil |

## Age & Birth Date

**Based on:**

* **BR-EM-01**: Employees must be at least 16 years old.

* **BR-EM-02**: Employees cannot be older than 100 years old.

* **BR-EM-03**: Birth dates cannot be in the future.

* **Section 5.1**: Year between 1926 and 2010 (relative to a current year of 2026).

### Equivalence Partitioning & BVA (3-point)

**Target Year:** 2026

| Field | Partition Type | Partition Range | Test Value | Boundary Values (3-point) | Test Case Values |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | :---: | ----- | ----- |
| **Age** | Invalid (Too young) | 0 – 15 | 10 | 15, 16 | 14, 15, 16 |
|  | Valid | 16 – 100 | 30 | 16, 100 | 15, 16, 17, 99, 100, 101 |
|  | Invalid (Too old) | 101+ | 110 | 100, 101 | 100, 101, 102 |
| **Birth Year** | Invalid (Future / Too young) | 2011 – MAX INT | 2035 | 2010, 2011 | 2009, 2010, 2011 |
|  | Valid | 1926 – 2010 | 1980 | 1926, 2010 | 1925, 1926, 1927, 2009, 2010, 2011 |
|  | Invalid (Too old) | \< 1926 | 1900 | 1925, 1926 | 1924, 1925, 1926 |

## Shift Duration & Timing

**Based on:**

* **FR-SH-07 / BR-SH-01**: End time must be after start time.

* **BR-WT-02**: At least 11 hours of rest between shifts.

* **FR-JR-05**: Employees should only be assigned shifts that match their job role.

### Decision Table — Shift Creation (Timing & Rules)

| Condition | Rule 1 | Rule 2 | Rule 4 | Rule 5 |
| ----- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| Start Time \< End Time? | T | F | T | T |
| Rest period ≥ 11 hours? | T | — | F | T |
| Matches Job Role? | T | — | — | F |
| Result | Valid | Error | Error | Error |

**Explanation:**

* **Rule 1:** Happy path — all conditions met, shift is created.

* **Rule 2:** Rejects when the user tries to end a shift before it starts.

* **Rule 4:** Enforces the 11-hour rest rule (BR-WT-02).

* **Rule 5:** Ensures the employee is qualified for the role (FR-JR-05).

## 3\. Weekly Working Hours

**Based on:**

* **BR-WT-01**: An employee must not work more than 37 hours per week on average.

### Equivalence Partitioning & BVA (2-point)

Assuming **a standard contract of 37 hours.**

| Value | Partition Type | Range | Test Value | Boundary | Test Case Values |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | :---: | ----- | ----- |
| **Weekly Hours** | Valid | 0.0 – 37.0 | 20.5 | 0.0, 37.0 | 0.0, 0.1, 36.9, 37.0 |
|  | Invalid (Overtime) | \> 37.0 | 40.0 | 37.1 | 37.1, 38.0 |

## Consecutive Work Days

**Based on:**

* **BR-WT-04**: Employees must not work more than 6 consecutive days.

* **BR-WT-05**: Employees must not work more than 3 consecutive night shifts.

### Equivalence Partitioning & BVA (3-point)

Night shifts are defined in Denmark as:  
Working at least 3 hours between the timeframe 22:00 \- 05:00

| Field | Partition Type | Range | Test Value | Boundary | Test Case Values |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Night work hours within 22:00–05:00 | Invalid | 0 – 2.99 hours | 2 hours | 3 hours | 2h 59m, 3h 00m, 3h 01m |
| Night work hours within 22:00–05:00 | Valid | 3 – 7 hours | 4 hours | 3 hours | 2h 59m, 3h 00m, 3h 01m |
| Shift outside night period | Invalid | 0 night hours | 0 hours | 22:00 / 05:00 | 14:00–22:00, 05:00–13:00 |
| Shift partly inside night period | Depends | 0 – 7 night hours | 3 hours | 3 hours | 21:00–00:00, 22:00–01:00, 03:00–06:00 |

### Equivalence Partitioning & BVA (3-point)

| Field | Partition Type | Range | Test Value | Boundary | Test Case Values |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | :---: | ----- | ----- |
| **Consecutive Days** | Valid | 1 – 6 | 3 | 1, 6 | 0, 1, 2, 5, 6, 7 |
|  | Invalid | 7+ | 8 | 7 | 6, 7, 8 |
| **Night Shifts** | Valid | 1 – 3 | 2 | 1, 3 | 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 |
|  | Invalid | 4+ | 5 | 4 | 3, 4, 5 |

## 5\. Shift Swap Approvals

**Based on:**

* **BR-AP-01**: Shift swaps must require manager or admin approval.

* **BR-AP-02**: An employee cannot approve their own swap request.

* **BR-AP-03**: A cancelled swap request cannot be approved.

* **BR-AP-04**: A declined swap request cannot be approved later.

* **BR-AP-05**: A swap involving a cancelled shift cannot be approved.

### Decision Table — Swap Approval

| Condition | Case 1 | Case 2 | Case 3 | Case 5 | Case 6 |
| ----- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| User is Admin/Manager? | T | F | T | T | T |
| Approver is not the Requester? | T | — | F | T | T |
| Request Status is 'Pending'? | T | — | — | F  | T |
| Shift is not cancelled? | T | — | — | — | F |
| Causes overlap? | F | — | — | — | — |
| Result | Approve | Deny | Deny | Deny | Deny |

**Explanation:**

* **Case 1:** The happy path — manager, not the requester, request is pending, shift is live, no overlap.

* **Case 2:** Regular employees cannot approve swaps (BR-AP-01).

* **Case 3:** Prevents self-approval (BR-AP-02).

* **Cases 5:** Cannot approve if already cancelled or declined (BR-AP-03, BR-AP-04).

* **Case 6:** Cannot swap a shift that has been cancelled (BR-AP-05).

## 9\. Deletion Constraints & Dependencies

**Based on:**

* **FR-EMP-05**: Prevent deletion of employees with future assigned shifts.

* **FR-JR-04**: Prevent deleting job roles linked to active employees or shifts.

### Decision Table — Resource Deletion

| Condition | Case 1 | Case 2 | Case 3 |
| ----- | :---: | :---: | :---: |
| Has active shifts / assignments? | Yes | No | No |
| Linked to active employees? | — | Yes | No |
| Result | Block | Block | Delete |

**Explanation:**

* Tests the referential-integrity rules. If any dependency exists (active shift or employee), the system must block deletion to prevent orphaned records.

## 10\. ISO 8601 Dates

**Based on:**.

* **BR-EM-04**: Birth dates must follow ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM).

* **BR-EM-05**: Date must be a real calendar date.

### Equivalence Partitioning — Date & Time Formats

| Field | Partition | Test Value | Result |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | :---: |
| **Date Format** | Valid ISO 8601 | 2024-05-12T00:00 | Valid |
|  | Invalid format | 12-05-2024T00:00 | Invalid |
|  | Invalid date | 2024-02-29T00:00  | Invalid (non-existent date) |
